openFindings.title: README still labels the Spec Kit section as Documentation instead of Framework
openFindings.scopeProof: README.md:33 and README.md:208 contain the old label; spec.md:78 and spec.md:113 require the rename and old-anchor cleanup.
openFindings.disposition_evidence: README.md:33 TOC entry and README.md:209 heading (plus anchor) both renamed to Spec Kit Framework; whole-repo grep confirms no remaining reference to the old label or anchor.

openFindings.title: Goal plugin is only documented as a Utility command instead of a full FEATURES subsection
openFindings.scopeProof: README.md:366/557/678/778 show comparable full feature sections; README.md:1230-1233 gives Goal only a Utility entry; plugin contract exists at .opencode/plugins/README.md:49; spec.md:78 and spec.md:135 require promotion.
openFindings.disposition_evidence: New README.md ### Goal Plugin FEATURES subsection added (with TOC entry) preserving the exact current Claude-Code-vs-OpenCode wording verbatim; old Commands > Utility bullet trimmed to a one-line cross-reference.

openFindings.title: Active graph metadata still indexes the retired Spec Kit Documentation label
openFindings.scopeProof: Whole-repo exact rg for '#spec-kit-documentation' found no live anchor consumer beyond README/spec/review evidence; however graph-metadata.json:164 still carries the retired entity and tasks.md:72 still contains the retired label as source text, so a metadata regeneration after only marking tasks complete is not proven to self-resolve this finding. The fix must explicitly update source wording and regenerate metadata, or manually correct graph-metadata after regeneration.
openFindings.disposition_evidence: tasks.md T008 reworded to avoid restating the retired label as a live entity; description.json/graph-metadata.json regenerated for this phase folder; grep confirms the retired entity no longer appears in either file.

openFindings.title: Root Deep Loop docs omit the fan-out permission and guardrail safety posture
openFindings.scopeProof: README.md:780 and README.md:817-818 advertise autonomous Deep Loop execution; reviewed README/AGENTS surfaces do not mention dangerously-skip-permissions, stall watchdog, cost cap, or lag-ceiling guardrails; fanout-run.cjs:1246-1251 and fanout-run.cjs:1280-1291 show native/cli-opencode permission-bypass behavior, with prompt-only lineageDir boundary warning.
affectedSurfaceHints: root README Deep Loop FEATURES section
openFindings.disposition_evidence: New "Bounded autonomy" bullet added directly in the README Deep Loop Runtime section, naming the permission/sandbox boundary and the stall watchdog, per-lineage cost cap and lag-ceiling guardrails.

openFindings.title: Deep-review iteration 5 has a body/final-line verdict mismatch
openFindings.scopeProof: Read iteration-5.md:45-52 and registry lines 6-70; mismatch is confined to iteration-5.md while iteration-6 body and final line both use CONDITIONAL.
openFindings.disposition_evidence: iteration-5.md's final line corrected from 'Review verdict: PASS' to 'Review verdict: CONDITIONAL', matching its own body text.
